diagrams.description: Sociology of Work examines the social organization, meaning, and consequences of work. This flowchart illustrates its analytical process: starting from the ultimate goal of understanding how work is structured and experienced, it integrates core dimensions (work organization, division of labor, labor process, employment relations, occupations, technology, workplace culture). Major theoretical lenses are then applied (Marxist alienation, Weberian rationalization, Durkheimian solidarity, feminist theory, interactionism, institutionalism). Through these, the field analyzes contemporary issues like precarity, emotional labor, deskilling, and the gig economy, ultimately producing outcomes such as labor market policies, union strategies, and improvements in quality of work life.
